note: Enhanced PR Freelance pipeline scrape. 7-day window. Web-search sourced only (no LinkedIn bot scraping). Headline theme this run: boutique financial-comms agency consolidation — the Haven Tower Alliance added two member firms in May 2026 (Conway 5/5, Cardinal 5/12), a textbook capacity-expansion moment for senior white-label / overflow support. Deduplicated against pr-freelance reports 2026-05-12/08/07/06/05/04/01 and daily-scrape 2026-05-13 (Adfin excluded — already captured in 5/13 daily as pr-marketing). All leads scored >=55 on the 100-point model.

leads.name: Cardinal Communications Strategies
leads.industry: Strategic Financial Communications Firm — Private Equity Sponsor Specialist
leads.compensationText: Not stated — agency-overflow / white-label engagements typically project + hourly + retainer
leads.employmentTypeRaw: Cold approach — senior agency-overflow / white-label support during alliance build-out
leads.notes: HEADLINE LEAD (combined 86, A grade). Cardinal Communications Strategies is a strategic financial-communications firm serving private equity sponsors across media relations, executive positioning, content strategy and digital marketing. On 2026-05-12 it announced an exclusive strategic alliance with Haven Tower Group — the 'Cardinal Haven Initiative' — to support PE firms investing in wealth management and the wealth-management enterprises they acquire. Both firms remain independent. A new joint go-to-market initiative plus a curated alliance network is a textbook capacity-expansion moment: senior, plug-in-fast white-label / overflow execution is exactly what these moments demand, and Cardinal's PE-sponsor + wealth-management remit is dead-center in Mark's ICP. ACTION: Direct outreach to Cardinal's principal positioning senior agency-overflow capacity — emphasize PE-sponsor narrative, portfolio-company comms, executive positioning, and ability to lead client-facing work without ramp time. Reference the Cardinal Haven Initiative specifically to show you're tracking the expansion.

leads.name: Haven Tower Group
leads.industry: Full-Service Communications Agency — Wealth Management Industry Specialist
leads.location: Los Angeles, Seattle, Chicago, New York City
leads.compensationText: Not stated — white-label / overflow engagements typically project + retainer
leads.employmentTypeRaw: Cold approach — senior white-label / overflow execution as the Haven Tower Alliance scales
leads.notes: Combined 84, A grade. Haven Tower Group is a multiple-award-winning, full-service communications agency dedicated exclusively to the wealth-management industry, with ~20 professionals across LA, Seattle, Chicago and NYC. It runs the Haven Tower Alliance (HTA), a curated community of independent agencies — and in May 2026 it added two members in quick succession (Conway Communications 5/5, Cardinal Communications 5/12). Rapid alliance build-out against a 20-person core team is a clear capacity signal: as deal flow flows through HTA, Haven Tower will need senior execution muscle it can trust on client-facing wealth-management work. ACTION: Direct outreach to Haven Tower leadership positioning senior overflow / white-label capacity specialized in wealth-management storytelling, media relations and executive comms. Note that you can plug into HTA-routed work without ramp. Mention the Conway and Cardinal alliances to demonstrate you understand their model.

leads.name: Conway Communications
leads.industry: Independent Boutique Investor Relations & Financial Communications Firm
leads.compensationText: Not stated — interim IR support engagements typically retainer or project
leads.employmentTypeRaw: Cold approach — interim IR / overflow support; firm explicitly markets 'interim IR support' as a service line
leads.notes: Combined 81, A grade. Conway Communications is a Boston-based boutique investor relations and financial-communications firm providing strategic IR, interim IR support and corporate communications counsel to public and private companies across financial services, technology and life sciences. Two things make this a strong fit: (1) the firm explicitly markets 'interim IR support' as a service line — meaning it is structurally comfortable with interim / fractional engagements and likely staffs them with senior contractors; (2) on 2026-05-05 it became the first inter-agency member of the Haven Tower Alliance, signaling growth and cross-referral volume. ACTION: Direct outreach to Conway's principal positioning senior interim IR / investor-narrative support — earnings comms, investor-day prep, transaction comms, first-time-public-company support. Lean on Mark's IR + capital-markets fluency. The 'interim IR' framing is your foot in the door — pitch yourself as exactly the senior bench they extend to clients.

leads.name: FINN Partners
leads.industry: Global Integrated Marketing & PR Agency — Financial Services PR Practice
leads.compensationText: Posted FS PR staff roles: AAE ~$24-25/hr; Senior AE commensurate w/ experience. Freelance overflow rates typically higher / project basis
leads.employmentTypeRaw: Mix — multiple full-time Financial Services PR roles open (Assistant AE, Senior AE); freelance/overflow demand inferred from active practice hiring

contacts.name: FINN Partners — Financial Services PR Practice Lead
contacts.title: Partner / Practice Lead, Financial Services

leads.notes: Combined 73, B grade. FINN Partners is a ~$200M independent agency with 1,300+ professionals across 35 offices and a dedicated Financial Services PR practice (B2B and B2C clients). It currently has multiple FS PR roles open — Assistant Account Executive and Senior Account Executive — with interviews running in 2026. The posted roles themselves are full-time staff hires (so direct intent for freelance is moderate), but a large agency actively building out an FS practice almost always carries parallel freelance / overflow demand it doesn't post publicly. ACTION: Skip the junior staff postings — instead do direct outreach to the FS PR practice lead positioning senior freelance / overflow capacity. Frame it as 'you're clearly scaling the FS practice; here's senior bench you can deploy on client-facing work immediately, project or retainer.' This is a relationship play, not an application.

leads.name: Flo Health Inc.
leads.compensationText: Not stated in posting — freelance / contract engagement
leads.employmentTypeRaw: Freelance — explicit 'PR & Media Relations Consultant (Freelance)' contract posting to support the US PR & Media team

contacts.name: Flo Health — US PR & Media Team
contacts.title: PR / Communications Lead

leads.notes: Combined 70, B grade. Flo Health is hiring a 'PR & Media Relations Consultant (Freelance)' on a contract basis to support its US PR & Media team — the single most explicit freelance PR posting surfaced this run. It scores B almost entirely on intent: clear freelance engagement model, remote US, direct media-relations remit, easy LinkedIn apply path. The catch is sector — Flo is consumer femtech, well outside Mark's finance / PE / B2B ICP, so fit is capped at 30 (no sector or buyer-quality points). Included because the task weights explicit freelance postings and the intent signal is genuinely strong. ACTION: Lower-priority but low-effort — worth a direct application given the explicit freelance framing and easy apply. Position media-relations execution capacity; don't over-invest unless they respond. Treat as a volume/keep-warm play, not a core-ICP pursuit.

leads.name: Rogo
leads.industry: Agentic AI Platform for Finance — Investment Banking & Private Equity Workflow Automation
leads.location: New York, NY + remote (global expansion underway)
leads.fundingStage: Series D (April 2026, $160M led by Kleiner Perkins; >$300M total raised)
leads.compensationText: Not stated — cold approach; fractional / project / retainer typical for post-Series-D scaling-narrative work
leads.employmentTypeRaw: Cold approach — fractional / project comms support for post-Series-D scaling narrative and thought leadership
leads.notes: Combined 58, C grade. Rogo builds agentic AI for investment bankers and PE firms (financial modeling, SEC-filing search, investment-memo drafting); 35,000+ finance professionals at 250+ institutions including Rothschild, Jefferies, Lazard, Moelis and Nomura use it. It raised a $160M Series D led by Kleiner Perkins (announced 2026-04-29, >$300M total) explicitly to fund global expansion and deeper financial-institution partnerships. Sector fit is excellent — investment banking / PE / fintech / AI is core ICP — but this is a cold-approach lead, not a posted role, and the funding news is ~2 weeks old (just outside the tight recency window), so intent scores modestly. The opportunity is a scaling-narrative + thought-leadership program: Series D victory lap, Felix (agentic AI) launch storytelling, enterprise/financial-institution case-study narrative, and an industry-event speaking calendar. ACTION: Cold outreach to Rogo's comms/marketing lead positioning senior fractional / project support on post-Series-D narrative and a thought-leadership program aimed at the IB/PE buyer. Mark's IB-adjacent fluency is the differentiator — he can speak the buyer's language.
